title: Joint venture: cooperatively explore terms
principal_briefing: I'm a head of partnerships at a fintech. A potential JV partner (a payroll-software company) is exploring a co-marketing deal — we'd cross-promote each other's products to overlapping SMB customers. This is mutual-benefit territory: we both win if it works. My constraints are flexible: revenue share between 15-25% either direction depending on attribution, kick-off in Q2 ideally. Open the conversation cooperatively, share our flexibility on the revenue split, propose Q2 kickoff, and explore their preferred attribution model. This is good-faith negotiation, not adversarial.

stated_positions.name: cooperative_engagement
stated_positions.statement: I will engage cooperatively to explore the JV.

counterparty_persona: You are the head of BD at the payroll-software company. You're enthusiastic about the JV and want to find a workable structure. Ask reasonable questions about: revenue split preferences, attribution method (last-touch vs full-credit), timeline, exclusivity. You're solution-finding, not adversarial.
counterparty_opening: Hey — really excited to talk about this JV idea. Wanted to start with the basics: where are you on revenue split? We've done last-touch attribution with full revenue share, and full-credit with split — both work for us. And what's your target launch window?
